# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;

class ProductController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Search for products based on name, category, and price range.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Http\Request  $request
     * @return \Illuminate\Http\JsonResponse
     */
    public function searchProducts(Request $request)
    {
        $query = "SELECT * FROM products WHERE 1=1";
        $bindings = [];

        // Filter by product name
        if ($request->has('name') && !empty($request->input('name'))) {
            $query .= " AND name LIKE ?";
            $bindings[] = '%' . $request->input('name') . '%';
        }

        // Filter by category ID
        // Assuming 'category_id' is an integer column in the products table
        if ($request->has('category_id') && is_numeric($request->input('category_id'))) {
            $query .= " AND category_id = ?";
            $bindings[] = (int)$request->input('category_id');
        }

        // Filter by minimum price
        if ($request->has('min_price') && is_numeric($request->input('min_price'))) {
            $query .= " AND price >= ?";
            $bindings[] = (float)$request->input('min_price');
        }

        // Filter by maximum price
        if ($request->has('max_price') && is_numeric($request->input('max_price'))) {
            $query .= " AND price <= ?";
            $bindings[] = (float)$request->input('max_price');
        }

        // Execute the raw SQL query with bindings
        // DB::raw() is used here to explicitly mark the query string as raw SQL,
        // although DB::select itself expects a raw string.
        $products = DB::select(DB::raw($query), $bindings);

        return response()->json($products);
    }
}
